demographics.living_situation: lives at home with a garden

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My arms are covered in giant blisters that itch so badly I feel like tearing my skin off. It's spreading everywhere.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came up overnight after I was clearing weeds and brush in the garden yesterday, and it's got much worse — the itching is unbearable, I couldn't sleep at all, and now there are big blisters that are bursting and weeping.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 24 hours ago, roughly half a day after I spent the afternoon clearing dense brush and weeds in the garden
symptom_narrative.duration: around a day, but it has got a lot worse and blistered up in the last several hours
symptom_narrative.character: intensely itchy and burning red skin that started as red patches and has come up in big tense blisters; some have burst and are oozing clear fluid and crusting over
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the itch is so bad I feel like tearing my skin off and I couldn't sleep a wink; the blisters and swelling make my hands hard to use
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the backs of both my forearms and hands, on both sides
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and getting worse since it started, worst overnight
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much has helped; a cool cloth takes the edge off the itch for a moment
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: scratching, warmth, and anything rubbing on the blisters makes the itch and burning worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no fever, no swelling of my face or lips, no trouble breathing, and no sores in my mouth or eyes — it's just the skin on my arms and hands
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had itchy red patches from plants before when gardening, but never blisters like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I couldn't sleep and I'm struggling to use my hands because they're blistered and swollen

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I'd been clearing thick weeds and brush in the garden the afternoon before this came up; my gardening gloves and tools are still by the back door

objective_data_offered.value: bright red intensely itchy skin on the backs of both forearms and hands, with the redness and blisters coming up in odd straight lines and streaks tracing where the plants brushed against me; there are lots of tense blisters and some bigger ones, and a few have burst leaving weeping crusted raw spots
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: described by the patient over video; not a clinician examination

objective_data_offered.value: it's on both arms about the same, on the backs of the forearms and hands where I was reaching into the brush — not on my face, body or legs
worn out and constantly scratching at my forearms after no sleep
I haven't taken my temperature but I don't feel hot, shivery or unwell in myself
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient impression only; no thermometer reading taken
